name: LEVI.SCT
program:
LEVI.SCT (Levi Schechtmann) is a genre-defying German pianist who is redefining the classical concert experience as we know it. He seems to effortlessly blend the emotional depth of classical composers with the raw energy of hip-hop and house music.

With his outstanding technique and his carefree approach to seemingly contrasting styles, LEVI.SCT is at the forefront of a new wave of artists who want to make classical music relevant and exciting for a new generation. With over 1,000,000 followers on Instagram, LEVI.SCT’s rise has been nothing short of phenomenal. Similar to his social media content, his concerts combine virtuosity, rhythm and emotion into a cinematic and immersive experience.

name: Mischpoke
program:
Mischpoke is a klezmer band from Hamburg that has long since gained an international audience. Anyone who experiences the electrifying performance of these five strong artistic personalities immediately feels the collective live energy that sets hearts and hips vibrating.

With »Tsukunft«, Mischpoke consistently continues its unmistakable sound of klezmer, jazz, world music, and classical music—and deliberately looks ahead. Traditional material is transformed and developed into an open, cross-genre sound language in which virtuosity, joy of playing, and tonal curiosity playfully intertwine. Whether in a small club or the Berlin Philharmonic Hall, Mischpoke draws the audience into a maelstrom of driving rhythms, poetic lyrics, delicate timbres, and unbridled improvisation. Mischpoke’s music is rooted in Jewish culture—a cultural context that is repeatedly interpreted politically in current debates. This makes it all the more important for the band to create a musical space in which diversity, contrasts, and friction are not seen as opposites, but as energy: lively, productive, and connecting.

performers: Cornelia Gottesleben violin Frank Naruga guitar Maria Rothfuchs double bass / Magdalena Abrams clarinet / Marta Winnitzki piano
